scope: The SwornAgent board command now discovers every locally available release board through one read-only catalog oracle, selects a deterministic topology source and farthest valid slice evidence, and reports explicit committed or uncommitted provenance while keeping named-board output compatible.

test_results.output_ref: journal.md completion entry — 2026-07-17 (6/6 acceptance checks covered)
journal.md completion entry — 2026-07-17 (structured PASS; no findings)

reachability.evidence: cmd/sworn/board_test.go:TestBoardCLIAllRefsCatalogStateEvidenceReachability compiles the CLI and runs `sworn board --json` from a temporary consumer Git repository whose checked-out HEAD has no release board. The fixture asserts two non-HEAD releases, deterministic source/state provenance, and byte-identical HEAD, branch, refs, porcelain status, and process directory snapshots. Mutation evidence: forcing discovery to use only HEAD made the test fail with `releases=0, want 2` (exit 1); after restoration, the same test passed (exit 0).

delivered.item: AC-01 — no-filter board discovery returns a bytewise release-sorted all-ref catalog, including non-HEAD releases, selected source refs, and farthest elected slice evidence.
AC-02 — topology source selection follows canonical local, canonical remote, local noncanonical, then remote noncanonical priority with bytewise tie-breaking.
AC-03 — malformed, missing, or identity-mismatched canonical records fail the catalog closed without partial output or fallback selection.
AC-04 — one shared state-evidence election selects lifecycle and attention high water marks and labels committed versus dirty-working-tree provenance.
AC-05 — named release queries use the same catalog evidence while retaining the established release/tracks JSON envelope and one-board text form.
AC-06 — ref discovery remains read-only and the full targeted and repository-wide Go gates are clean.
